description:
You are a research assistant who just received two small comma-separated data dumps.  
Both files already exist on disk:

• /home/user/datasets/raw/experiment_A.csv  
• /home/user/datasets/raw/experiment_B.csv  

Each file has the exact same structure:

ID,Value  
<alphanumeric-id>,<number or the string "N/A">

Concrete file contents

----- /home/user/datasets/raw/experiment_A.csv -----
ID,Value
A1,10
A2,15
A3,N/A
A4,20
----------------------------------------------------

----- /home/user/datasets/raw/experiment_B.csv -----
ID,Value
B1,5
B2,8
B3,11
B4,N/A
B5,12
----------------------------------------------------

Task you must perform (use only standard GNU tools; rely chiefly on awk and sed):

1. Create the directory /home/user/datasets/clean.  
   Inside it generate one “cleaned” CSV for every original file, using *only* awk/sed
   (no python, csvkit, etc.).  Name them experiment_A_clean.csv and
   experiment_B_clean.csv respectively.  The rules for a cleaned CSV are:

   1.1  The header must be lower-case exactly “id,value” (no spaces).  
   1.2  Any data line whose Value field is the literal string “N/A” must be removed.  
   1.3  All other lines must be preserved unchanged except for the header case fix.

2. Create the file /home/user/datasets/summary.tsv containing a **tab-separated**
   statistical report with the following four columns, in exactly this order:

       file	total_rows	valid_rows	mean_value

   • total_rows – number of data lines (excluding the header) in the *original* file.  
   • valid_rows – number of lines kept in the cleaned version.  
   • mean_value – arithmetic mean of the numeric Value entries kept, printed with
     exactly two digits after the decimal point (use awk’s printf).  

   The file must have a header line plus three data lines in this exact order:
   experiment_A.csv, experiment_B.csv, and an aggregate line called “ALL” that
   combines the numbers of both datasets.

   Example of the required layout (values here are placeholders; the automated test
   will check the real numbers):

   file<TAB>total_rows<TAB>valid_rows<TAB>mean_value
   experiment_A.csv<TAB>…	…	…
   experiment_B.csv<TAB>…	…	…
   ALL<TAB>…	…	…

   (<TAB> above means a real tab character, *not* spaces.)

3. To help future reproducibility, keep a plain-text log of every awk and sed command
   you execute.  
   • Before running any command, first echo the exact command line to
     /home/user/datasets/logs/process_log.txt (create the logs directory if needed).  
   • Then run the command normally so that the cleaning actually happens.  
   The log must therefore list each command on its own line in the exact order you
   executed them.  No other commentary is allowed in the log.

Summary:
• Two cleaned CSV files must appear under /home/user/datasets/clean/.  
• One tab-separated summary must appear at /home/user/datasets/summary.tsv.  
• A command log must appear at /home/user/datasets/logs/process_log.txt.

Automated grading will verify:
• File and directory names match exactly.  
• Cleaned CSVs follow rules 1.1–1.3.  
• summary.tsv contains precisely calculated statistics with correct formatting and
  tab separation.  
• process_log.txt contains at least one sed and one awk invocation and no extra text.

Do not remove, rename or overwrite the original files.  Good luck!
truth:
Expected final artefacts and their exact contents
================================================

1. /home/user/datasets/clean/experiment_A_clean.csv
---------------------------------------------------
id,value
A1,10
A2,15
A4,20

(ending with a single trailing newline)

2. /home/user/datasets/clean/experiment_B_clean.csv
---------------------------------------------------
id,value
B1,5
B2,8
B3,11
B5,12

(trailing newline)

3. /home/user/datasets/summary.tsv
----------------------------------
file	total_rows	valid_rows	mean_value
experiment_A.csv	4	3	15.00
experiment_B.csv	5	4	9.00
ALL	9	7	11.57

(trailing newline)

4. /home/user/datasets/logs/process_log.txt  
   – must exist and be readable.  
   – must contain at least the two substrings “awk ” and “sed ” (space required
     after the command name) somewhere in the file.  
   – each non-blank line must be a command that was actually executed.  
   – exact commands are not enforced by the test, only that the log is non-empty
     and mentions both awk and sed.

Pre-existing directory structure before the agent begins
--------------------------------------------------------
/home/user/datasets/raw/experiment_A.csv   (contains data shown in the public task)
/home/user/datasets/raw/experiment_B.csv   (contains data shown in the public task)

No other files or directories under /home/user/datasets/ exist at start.

Permissions
-----------
All directories under /home/user are writable by the current user.
